evals.prompt: I'm building a dungeon level for my fantasy game and I need a sprite for a treasure chest. I don't have any art or reference for it yet, can you make me one? It should fit with the rest of my purple-blue sprite set.
evals.expected_output: A small (16-24px) treasure chest sprite PNG with transparent background, colors from the house palette, sourced via openly-licensed reference images rather than hand-drawn pixels.
assertions: A sprite PNG file is delivered in the outputs directory
The sprite has a transparent background (all four corner pixels are fully transparent)
The sprite is item-sized: longest side is between 8 and 32 pixels
The sprite uses at most 16 unique opaque colors (pixel-art palette discipline)
Every opaque color in the sprite is an exact color from the house palette (assets/palette.txt)
Alpha is hard: every pixel is fully opaque or fully transparent (no semi-transparent fringe)
If web images were used as reference, attribution/license information is preserved in the outputs

evals.prompt: i took this photo of a candle: /Users/jelle.vanelburg/fantasy-sprites/eval-workspace/test-inputs/my_candle_photo.jpg - can you turn it into a sprite for the tavern scenes in my game? its a small prop that sits on tables so it shouldnt be big
evals.expected_output: A small item sprite (around 16-24px) converted from the photo, background removed, hard alpha, house palette colors.
assertions: A sprite PNG file is delivered in the outputs directory
The photo background was removed: all four corner pixels are fully transparent
The sprite is small-prop-sized: longest side is between 8 and 32 pixels
The sprite uses at most 16 unique opaque colors (pixel-art palette discipline)
Every opaque color in the sprite is an exact color from the house palette (assets/palette.txt)
Alpha is hard: every pixel is fully opaque or fully transparent (no semi-transparent fringe)

evals.prompt: Here's a render of a castle for my game: /Users/jelle.vanelburg/fantasy-sprites/eval-workspace/test-inputs/castle_render.png. Turn it into a building sprite, and I also need an SVG version because my engine scales background assets.
evals.expected_output: A building-sized sprite (around 64px) PNG plus a valid SVG version with crisp pixel edges, both using the house palette.
assertions: A sprite PNG file is delivered in the outputs directory
An SVG file is delivered and is well-formed XML with an <svg> root element
The SVG preserves crisp pixel edges (shape-rendering crispEdges or equivalent integer-aligned rects)
The PNG sprite is building-sized: longest side is between 48 and 128 pixels
The sprite uses at most 16 unique opaque colors (pixel-art palette discipline)
Every opaque color in the sprite is an exact color from the house palette (assets/palette.txt)
Alpha is hard: every pixel is fully opaque or fully transparent (no semi-transparent fringe)
